<!--
Thanks for contributing to CWG! Fill in the sections below.
See docs/CONTRIBUTING.md for branch naming, commit format, and testing guidance.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do, and why? -->

## Type of change

<!-- Match your commit prefix (see docs/CONTRIBUTING.md → Commit Format). Check one. -->

- [ ] `feat` — New feature
- [ ] `fix` — Bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — Documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` — Code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- [ ] `test` — Adding or updating tests
- [ ] `ci` — CI/CD changes
- [ ] `chore` — Maintenance, dependencies, tooling
- [ ] Breaking change (`feat!` / `fix!`)

## Related issues

<!-- e.g. Closes #13 -->

## Testing

<!--
CI runs `pytest` across all supported Python versions on every PR, so no need
to confirm the suite here. Describe any *manual* verification CI can't do —
e.g. `cwg run <sample.cwg>` against a known-good program. See
docs/CONTRIBUTING.md → Testing.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] Branch name follows the convention (`feat/`, `fix/`, `docs/`, …)
- [ ] Commits use conventional commit prefixes
- [ ] Docs updated if behaviour or commands changed
